---
title: "HEOR"
output: html_HEOR
---

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code. 

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Ctrl+Shift+Enter*. 

```{r}
install.packages("readr")
library(readr)

zip_path <- "C:/Users/rashidiranjn/Documents/HEOR/h251dat.zip"

meps_path <- unzip(zip_path, exdir = "C:/Users/rashidiranjn/Documents/HEOR")

source("C:/Users/rashidiranjn/Documents/HEOR/h251ru.txt")

head(h251)
dim(h251)
names(h251)[1:100]
summary(h251$TOTEXP23)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)

heor <- h251 %>%
  select(
    DUPERSID,
    AGE23X,
    SEX,
    COGLIM31,
    TOTEXP23,
    TOTSLF23,
    ERTOT23,
    IPTOFD23,
    OBTOTV23,
    PERWT23F,
    VARSTR,
    VARPSU
  )
```

```{r}

table(heor$COGLIM31)
 #1 = Yes, cognitive limitation
 #2 = No, no cognitive limitation
#-1 = Inapplicable
#-8 = Don't know / refused / not ascertained-type missing

heor_cog <- heor %>%  filter(COGLIM31 %in% c(1, 2)) %>%
  mutate(
    coglim_group = case_when(
      COGLIM31 == 1 ~ "Cognitive limitation",
      COGLIM31 == 2 ~ "No cognitive limitation"
    ) ,
    female = if_else(SEX == 2, 1, 0)
  )  %>%  select(-COGLIM31, -SEX)

table1_unweighted <- heor_cog %>%
  group_by(coglim_group) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_age = mean(AGE23X, na.rm = TRUE),
    female_percent = mean(female, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    mean_total_cost = mean(TOTEXP23, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_out_of_pocket = mean(TOTSLF23, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_er_visits = mean(ERTOT23, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_inpatient_events = mean(IPTOFD23, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_office_visits = mean(OBTOTV23, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

table1_unweighted

```
In this unweighted MEPS 2023 analysis, individuals reporting cognitive limitation had higher mean annual healthcare expenditures, higher out-of-pocket costs, and more emergency room visits than individuals without cognitive limitation. They were also older on average and more likely to be female. These descriptive findings suggest a higher healthcare utilization and cost burden among people with cognitive limitation.


```{r}
library(survey)
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)
```
```{r survey-design}
options(survey.lonely.psu = "adjust")

#id      = cluster / PSU
#strata  = survey stratum
#weights = person-level weight
#data    = your cleaned analysis dataset

meps_design <- svydesign(
  id = ~VARPSU,
  strata = ~VARSTR,
  weights = ~PERWT23F,
  data = heor_cog,
  nest = TRUE
)

```


```{r weighted-n}
svytable(~coglim_group, design = meps_design)

weighted_table <- svyby(
  ~AGE23X + female + TOTEXP23 + TOTSLF23 + ERTOT23,
  ~coglim_group,
  design = meps_design,
  FUN = svymean,
  na.rm = TRUE
)

weighted_table_clean <- data.frame(
  coglim_group = weighted_table$coglim_group,
  mean_age = weighted_table$AGE23X,
  female_percent = weighted_table$female * 100,
  mean_total_cost = weighted_table$TOTEXP23,
  mean_out_of_pocket = weighted_table$TOTSLF23,
  mean_er_visits = weighted_table$ERTOT23
)

knitr::kable(weighted_table_clean, digits = 2)
```

In the weighted MEPS 2023 analysis, individuals reporting cognitive limitation had substantially higher annual healthcare expenditures than those without cognitive limitation ($21,196 vs. $8,033). They also had higher mean out-of-pocket costs ($1,815 vs. $1,078) and greater emergency room utilization (0.62 vs. 0.19 visits per person-year). The cognitive limitation group was older on average and had a higher proportion of female respondents. These findings suggest that cognitive limitation is associated with a substantially higher healthcare cost and utilization burden in the U.S. civilian noninstitutionalized population represented by MEPS.
